TSG Platform AWS Deployment Build

This guide describes the AWS deployment package for running TSG Platform as a production container on AWS App Runner with PostgreSQL and AWS Secrets Manager.

The package is produced by scripts/build_aws_deployment.ps1 and includes the Docker app, AWS CloudFormation template, example parameters, and paired Markdown/HTML documentation.

Included files

  • deploy/aws/cloudformation-app-runner.yml
  • deploy/aws/app-runner-parameters.example.json
  • Dockerfile
  • docker-compose.prod.yml
  • Application source under api, core, database, seed_data, and scripts
  • .env.production.example
  • Deployment documentation in Markdown and HTML
  • aws-manifest.json

Build the AWS package

powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\build_aws_deployment.ps1

The default output is:

dist\aws\TSG-Platform-AWS-0.2.0-rc.1.zip
dist\aws\TSG-Platform-AWS-0.2.0-rc.1.zip.sha256

To also build the Docker image locally:

powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\build_aws_deployment.ps1 -BuildImage

Container image flow

aws ecr create-repository --repository-name tsg-platform
aws ecr get-login-password --region us-east-1 | docker login --username AWS --password-stdin 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com
docker build --tag tsg-platform:0.2.0-rc.1 .
docker tag tsg-platform:0.2.0-rc.1 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tsg-platform:0.2.0-rc.1
docker push 123456789012.dkr.ecr.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/tsg-platform:0.2.0-rc.1

Use the pushed image URI as the CloudFormation ImageIdentifier parameter.

Required secrets

SecretValue
DATABASE_URLPostgreSQL connection string
TSG_AUTH_SECRETRandom secret, at least 32 characters
TSG_LICENSE_SIGNING_SECRETRandom secret, at least 32 characters
TSG_CLIENT_API_SECRETRandom secret, at least 32 characters
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRETReal Stripe webhook signing secret beginning with whsec_
SMTP_PASSWORDSMTP account password
aws secretsmanager create-secret --name tsg-platform/auth-secret --secret-string "REPLACE_WITH_32_PLUS_RANDOM_CHARS"

The CloudFormation template receives secret ARNs, not raw secret values.

Database

The application requires PostgreSQL for production:

TSG_ENV=production
TSG_DB_BACKEND=postgres

Use Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL or another managed PostgreSQL endpoint. If the database is private, create an App Runner VPC connector and pass its ARN as VpcConnectorArn. If VpcConnectorArn is empty, App Runner uses default public egress.

postgresql://tsg_user:STRONG_PASSWORD@database-hostname:5432/tsg_platform

Deploy CloudFormation

Copy and edit deploy/aws/app-runner-parameters.example.json, then create the stack:

aws cloudformation create-stack `
  --stack-name tsg-platform-prod `
  --template-body file://deploy/aws/cloudformation-app-runner.yml `
  --capabilities CAPABILITY_IAM `
  --parameters file://deploy/aws/app-runner-parameters.example.json

For subsequent template or parameter changes, use aws cloudformation update-stack with the same template and parameters, or use aws cloudformation deploy with inline ParameterKey=ParameterValue overrides.

Production configuration enforced by the app

  • TSG_AUTH_SECRET, TSG_LICENSE_SIGNING_SECRET, and TSG_CLIENT_API_SECRET are non-placeholder secrets of at least 32 characters.
  • TSG_DB_BACKEND=postgres.
  • DATABASE_URL is present.
  • STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET is a real Stripe signing secret.
  • TSG_SECURE_COOKIES=true.
  • TSG_ALLOWED_HOSTS contains explicit hostnames and does not include *.

Verify deployment

curl https://license.example.com/health
curl https://license.example.com/ready

Expected readiness response:

{
  "status": "ready",
  "checks": {
    "database": "ok"
  }
}

Run smoke checks for admin login, customer portal routing, product activation, update checks, Stripe webhook signature validation, and SMTP password setup email delivery.

Release evidence

  • ECR image URI and digest
  • CloudFormation stack ID
  • App Runner service URL and service ARN
  • RDS endpoint and database name
  • Secrets Manager ARNs
  • /health response
  • /ready response
  • Smoke-test timestamp and operator name
  • DNS and TLS configuration
